senses.definition: A dialect or historical term meaning laws, decrees, or official orders; sometimes used to refer to legal or formal rules in a bygone context.
examples: The blaws of the realm were written in old-time charters.
Scholars study the blaws to understand medieval governance.

senses.definition: A film genre from the 1970s that used Black actors and themes, often with sensational violence and explicit content; intended to appeal to urban Black audiences.
examples: Blaxploitation films brought new Black icons to screens in the 1970s.
Critics discuss both the empowerment and stereotypes in blaxploitation.

senses.definition: The actions or instances of exploiting Black people in film production; used to discuss the concept of blaxploitation in plural form.
examples: Scholars debate the blaxploitations of early cinema.
Documentaries examine various blaxploitations across different studios.

senses.definition: A few dialectal or arhaic spellings of 'bile' or 'blay' as a name, sometimes used as a surname or given name; rare in modern general usage.
examples: The village records mention a Blay who signed the charter.
Blay is a rare given name in some regions.

senses.definition: Describing something pale or wan in color or appearance; used occasionally in dialectal or poetic language.
examples: The blay sky hinted at an early frost.
A blay light fell across the room.

senses.definition: A given name or brand variant, often a phonetic spelling of the surname Blake; used as a personal name or brand name.
examples: Blayk released a new album this year.
She chose Blayk as her stage name.

senses.definition: A given name; variant spellings exist for Blayne or Blaine, often used as a first name.
examples: Blayne attended the conference on language learning.
The character Blayne appears in the novel.

senses.pos: noun (plural of blay/blue? or archaic plural form)
senses.definition: Plural form in dialect or name contexts; may appear in historical texts or as surname plurals.
examples: The Blays of the old family kept meticulous records.

senses.definition: A very bright, variable extragalactic object that emits jets of radiation from a supermassive black hole, often used in astronomy.
examples: The blazar’s brightness changed rapidly over weeks.
Astronomers study blazars to understand black holes.

senses.definition: Multiple bright extragalactic jet-emitting objects observed in astronomy.
examples: Several blazars were detected in the survey.
Blazars help scientists study jet physics.

senses.definition: To burn brightly or to light something on fire; to shine strongly.
examples: The campfire will blaze all night.
A car’s headlights blaze through the fog.

senses.definition: A bright flame or a blaze of light; a strong, noticeable fire.
examples: The blaze damaged several buildings.
Firefighters battled the blaze for hours.

senses.definition: Burned brightly or was on fire in the past.
examples: The house was blazing after the spark.
The investigator blazed through the report.

senses.definition: A lightweight jacket that is part of formal or semi-formal wear.
examples: She wore a navy blazer to the interview.
The blazer matched her skirt perfectly.

senses.definition: Wearing a blazer; dressed in a blazer.
examples: He arrived at the party blazered in a checked coat.
A blazered group walked into the meeting.

senses.definition: More than one blazer; jackets worn for smart-casual or formal occasions.
examples: The students wore matching blazers for the ceremony.
Stores sell blazers in many colors.

senses.definition: Bright flames or lines of light; a rapid display of brightness.
examples: The forest lit up with dozens of blazes from the campfires.
The lighthouse emitted quick blazes of light.

senses.definition: Burning very brightly; very fast or intense.
examples: The blazing sun made the day feel hot.
She walked with blazing speed to the finish line.

senses.definition: In a very bright, fast, or intense way.
examples: The fire burned blazingly in the night.
The team blazingly completed the task.

senses.definition: To display publicly or proclaim boldly; to decorate or inscribe in a visible way.
examples: The shield is blazoned with a red cross.
The hall was blazoned with the family motto.

senses.definition: A formal heraldic description or emblem on a shield or coat of arms.
examples: The blazon describes the coat of arms in precise terms.
Heralds study the blazon to understand lineage.

senses.definition: Decorated or shown publicly with heraldic or prominent markings; described in detail.
examples: A blazoned shield hung on the wall.
Her blazoned banner marked the team's identity.

senses.definition: More than one blazer jacket.
examples: She bought two blue blazers for the club.
The team wore their blazers at the ceremony.

senses.definition: Multiple bright flames or lines of light; rapid flashes.
examples: The blazes from the campfire danced in the night.
Searchlights sent blazes across the sky.

senses.definition: Very bright or very fast; extremely hot or intense.
examples: The blazing heat makes walking unbearable.
He drove with blazing speed.

senses.definition: Obsolete or rare term referring to the act of blazoning or the heraldic description; not common in modern use.
examples: Medieval books mention blazonment in heraldry.

senses.definition: Plural form referring to collections or instances of heraldic blazons; historical term in heraldry.
examples: The blazonries list the family arms.
